codeReview.summary: Solid implementation that covers all feature requirements. The page.tsx uses generateStaticParams for SSG, generateMetadata for SEO, and proper notFound() handling. PlanDetailContent.tsx is a well-structured client component with hero, description, What's Included grid, pricing toggle (subscription/one-time with 3-day/5-day options), CTA, FAQ accordion, and reviews. Data model extensions in types.ts and constants.ts are clean and comprehensive. All coding conventions from AGENTS.md are followed: zero border-radius, Tailwind utility classes, Next.js Image with data-placeholder, Button as='a' for CTAs, named exports, feature-specific component directory, and proper use of design tokens. Two minor non-blocking observations noted.

issues.description: The '/ week' unit label on pricing cards shows for both subscription and one-time views. For one-time purchases, this could be slightly confusing to users — it technically means 'per week of meals' but reads like a recurring charge. Consider adding a subtle note like 'one-time purchase' below the price when pricingView is 'one-time', or changing the label to something like '/ delivery'. Very minor UX polish.

The hero image uses an Unsplash URL directly in the Image component's src prop with fill mode. While this works with Next.js image optimization (Unsplash is likely in next.config.ts remotePatterns), if the Unsplash photo is removed the hero will break with no fallback. This is a known project-wide consideration (documented in AGENTS.md), not specific to this feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker used `Button as='a'` with plain `<a>` tags for internal navigation (e.g., linking to /meal-plans/order?plan=...). This means clicking the CTA triggers a full page reload instead of client-side navigation via Next.js router. The AGENTS.md convention says 'Prefer Button as=a href over nesting Link+Button', which is correct for avoiding invalid HTML, but doesn't mention the trade-off of losing SPA navigation. This is the established project-wide pattern (used in 9+ components), so it's consistent but could be noted as a future optimization opportunity.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/meal-plans/PlanDetailContent.tsx lines 200, 217, 238 all use Button as='a'. Same pattern in PlanComparisonCards.tsx, MealPlansCTA.tsx, OrderConfigurator.tsx, etc.

summary: The meal-plan-detail-pages feature passes review. All 6 expected behaviors are implemented: hero with plan data, What's Included section with detailed items, pricing with 3-day/5-day and subscription/one-time toggle, FAQ accordion using the existing component, CTA linking to order page with plan parameter, and responsive layout via Tailwind responsive classes. The implementation follows all project conventions (zero border-radius, design tokens, component organization, TypeScript strict mode, no any types). Two non-blocking issues noted: the '/ week' label persists in one-time pricing view (minor UX ambiguity), and Unsplash image fragility (known project-wide consideration). Build, typecheck, and lint all passed per handoff. Interactive verification was thorough with 10 checks covering both plans, all sections, toggle interactivity, and console error checks.
